Mathematical functions and constants for trigonometry, exponentiation, rounding, and IEEE arithmetic.

## Folders
- **[include](include/README.md)**: Header file for mathematical constants and functions for trigonometry, exponentiation, and rounding.

## Files
- **[acos.c](acos.c.md)**: Implementation of the arccosine function for double precision floating-point numbers.
- **[asin.c](asin.c.md)**: Implementation of the arcsine function using a rational approximation method.
- **[atan.c](atan.c.md)**: Implementation of the arctangent function using polynomial approximation and argument reduction.
- **[atan2.c](atan2.c.md)**: Implementation of the `atan2` function for computing the angle between the positive x-axis and the point (x, y).
- **[ceil.c](ceil.c.md)**: Implementation of the `ceil` function to round a double value toward negative infinity.
- **[copysign.c](copysign.c.md)**: Implements the copysign function to return a value with the magnitude of x and the sign of y.
- **[exp.c](exp.c.md)**: Implementation of the exponential function for double precision floating-point numbers.
- **[fabs.c](fabs.c.md)**: Function to return the absolute value of a double-precision floating-point number.
- **[finite.c](finite.c.md)**: Function to check if a double-precision floating-point number is finite.
- **[floor.c](floor.c.md)**: Implements the floor function to round a double toward negative infinity using bit manipulation.
- **[fmod.c](fmod.c.md)**: Implementation of the fmod function to compute the floating-point remainder of x divided by y.
- **[isnan.c](isnan.c.md)**: Function to check if a double is NaN (Not a Number) without branching.
- **[jerry-libm-internal.h](jerry-libm-internal.h.md)**: Header file for internal mathematical functions and macros supporting IEEE arithmetic in little-endian systems.
- **[log.c](log.c.md)**: Implementation of a logarithm function for double precision floating-point numbers.
- **[nextafter.c](nextafter.c.md)**: A C function to compute the next representable floating-point value after a given number.
- **[pow.c](pow.c.md)**: Implementation of the power function `pow(x, y)` for computing x raised to the power of y.
- **[scalbn.c](scalbn.c.md)**: Function to compute x multiplied by 2 raised to the power of n using exponent manipulation.
- **[sqrt.c](sqrt.c.md)**: Implementation of a function to compute the square root of a double-precision floating-point number using bit-by-bit integer arithmetic.
- **[trig.c](trig.c.md)**: Trigonometric functions for sine, cosine, and tangent with argument reduction and kernel functions.
